examples.title: Character Extraction with charAt()
examples.description: Program to extract and display each character along with its index using charAt()
examples.explanation: The charAt() method returns the character at the specified index. The loop iterates from 0 to length()-1, which is the valid range. Character arithmetic is possible because char is internally an unsigned 16-bit integer.

examples.title: Substring and Character Array Extraction
examples.description: Demonstrates substring() and toCharArray() methods for extracting portions of strings
examples.explanation: substring() returns a new String object containing the specified portion. toCharArray() creates a new char array with all characters. getChars() copies a range to an existing array - more memory efficient for large strings.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for substring and toCharArray, O(k) for getChars where k is range
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for substring and toCharArray, O(k) for getChars

examples.title: Counting Character Frequencies
examples.description: Program to count frequency of each character using character extraction methods
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates character extraction using charAt() in a loop. The LinkedHashMap maintains insertion order. The Java 8+ chars() method returns an IntStream of character codes, demonstrating modern character processing.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is number of unique characters
